channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Google loses fight over record $4.7 billion EU antitrust fine
item.description: In 2018, the European Commission slapped Google with the record-breaking penalty on the grounds that it abused Android's mobile dominance.

item.title: Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix shares tumble over 9% as chip rout spreads from Wall Street
item.description: Shares of Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix plummeted more than 7% in early Thursday trading.

item.title: Russia launches massive strike on Ukraine as Poland scrambles jets, Finland restricts airspace
item.description: The Russian defence ministry said that it launched a "massive strike using long-range precision air, land, and sea-based weapons and attack drones."

item.title: OpenAI proposes 5% stake to Trump administration to ease Washington pressure: report
item.description: Trump said in June that the U.S. taking an ownership stake in AI giants would be "a beautiful thing" and make American public "partners in this revolution."

item.title: M&A is ‘on fire’ as large-cap firms simplify, Citi UK CEO says
item.description: Citi U.K. CEO Tiina Lee said U.K. M&A is “on fire” as companies simplify and overseas buyers target British assets.

item.title: Volkswagen braces for boardroom showdown over historic cost-cutting plan
item.description: Volkswagen is poised for a boardroom showdown following reports that the auto giant is weighing up shutting four factories and implementing 100,000 job cuts.

item.title: Fed Chief Kevin Warsh declines to hint at July rate decision, but says inflation 'too high'
item.description: The Fed chairman joined a panel of other central bank leaders Wednesday at the ECB Forum on Central Banking in Sintra, Portugal.

item.title: The dollar looks ready to break higher. Here's why Wall Street is turning bullish
item.description: The U.S. dollar may be emerging from nearly a year of stagnation, with analysts increasingly arguing that the world's reserve currency is poised for a more sustained advance.

item.title: Amazon is designing its own AI chips for Echo, Fire TV and future devices, exec tells CNBC
item.description: Amazon hardware chief Panos Panay says the company is designing custom chips for key devices as it experiments with AI gadgets.

item.title: U.S. won’t renew USMCA, opening door for negotiations with Canada and Mexico
item.description: President Donald Trump's "primary" issue with USMCA centers on America's trade deficits with Canada and Mexico, a senior administration official said.

item.title: Tank maker KNDS postpones IPO amid market struggles for defense
item.description: Shareholders of the Amsterdam-based company have decided the process to IPO will proceed "upon the return of more favorable market conditions," KNDS said.

item.title: Lamborghini reveals new Urus performance hybrid SUV after ditching EVs
item.description: Lamborghini is calling the new Urus SE Performante the "fastest Super SUV in the world."

item.title: Meta says WhatsApp usernames are safeguarded against scams after India flags cybersecurity risks
item.description: Meta has defended its WhatsApp username feature after the Indian government raised cybersecurity concerns.

item.title: Shares of BYD and Xiaomi surge as June delivery figures fuel optimism
item.description: Hong Kong-listed shares of Chinese electric vehicle makers surged, after companies posted June vehicle data that helped to buoy investor sentiment.

item.title: Oil prices on pace for fourth straight weekly loss as Trump sees progress in U.S.-Iran talks
item.description: Oil prices fell more than 1% on Wednesday after talks between Washington and Tehran concluded.

item.title: EBay doesn't need Ryan Cohen for its comeback. How to profit from the turnaround
item.description: Tony Zhang breaks down how he's using options to capitalize on a comeback in eBay.

item.title: Europe wants to rebalance trade with Beijing, but can't quit Chinese air conditioners
item.description: An historic heat wave has driven unprecedented demand for Chinese-made air conditioners in Europe, underscoring the tough task Brussels faces in rebalancing trade with Beijing.

item.title: Amazon devices chief Panos Panay says AI is moving beyond screens and apps
item.description: Amazon's SVP of devices, Alexa and Leo, Panos Panay joins Arjun Kharpal to discuss how artificial intelligence is reshaping the company's hardware strategy, from Alexa and Echo devices to wearables, smart glasses, robotaxis and satellites.

item.title: Trump got $15,000 in FIFA tickets from Gianni Infantino. Next up: Presenting the World Cup trophy
item.description: Trump’s ties to FIFA and Gianni Infantino face new scrutiny as the World Cup enters the knockout round and European lawmakers press over a Peace Prize complaint.

item.title: Meta's plan to launch a cloud business eases the biggest overhang on the stock
item.description: The stock's struggles in 2026 had been due to concerns about its aggressive AI spending.

item.title: Dan Ives goes bullish on SpaceX, touting reusable, super heavy Starship rocket as linchpin for success
item.description: The rocket and satellite communications manufacturer first soared, and then fell back toward its $135 debut price in June.

item.title: Amazon Prime's 50-cent-per-gallon gas discount starts July 2. Here's how to maximize it
item.description: Amazon is offering ways for Prime members to save on gas, groceries and more from July 2 through July 6.

item.title: Alibaba-affiliate Ant Group rushes into humanoid robots with a dozen deals in 18 months
item.description: Ant has led a 500 million yuan ($73.59 million) funding round in humanoid robotics company Zeroth, the start-up announced Thursday.

item.title: Inside India newsletter: Amazon, Walmart-owned Flipkart get ready to shake upIndia's delivery-in-minutes sector
item.description: Amazon and Walmart-owned Flipkart are scrambling to expand quick commerce operations in India, not just to chase growth but to remain relevant to consumers.

item.title: Employers who laid off workers citing AI are already starting to regret it
item.description: Companies are realizing artificial intelligence can't do everything after all, prompting them to rehire employees to grow their businesses

item.title: Thursday's big stock stories: What’s likely to move the market in the next trading session
item.description: The Dow hit a record before closing marginally lower on Wednesday. Tune into CNBC Thursday at 5 p.m. ET for an exclusive interview with President Donald Trump.

item.title: Trump Accounts for kids launch July 4: What parents need to know
item.description: Here's a breakdown of how Trump Accounts work, who qualifies for free money and when the funds can be withdrawn.

item.title: UBS sees opportunities in these defensive stocks. They also pay dividends
item.description: UBS thinks these dividend-paying defensive names are poised to move higher.

item.title: Why Japanese airlines' $400 fuel surcharges are hitting now — and when they will end
item.description: Fuel surcharges at Japan Airlines and All Nippon Airways increased Wednesday, despite falling jet fuel costs in Asia.

item.title: South Korean government discriminated against Coupang, U.S. companies, House report finds
item.description: The House Judiciary Committee said the South Korean government discriminated against Coupang and other U.S. companies, in a new report.
